crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Above $64K as Crypto Market Adds $170B in July Despite Recent Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin is trading around $63,950–$64,200, up approximately 1% over 24 hours, while the total crypto market has gained $170B since July 1st despite a record $19B liquidation event in the prior 24-hour window.

items.title: Ethereum Drops 40% and Major Altcoins Face Sharp Liquidations Amid Market Turbulence
items.summary: Ethereum fell 40% in a major liquidation event that wiped out $19B in 24 hours—the largest on record—while BTC, ETH, SOL, and XRP saw significant liquidations totaling $63.76M affecting over 44,000 traders.

items.title: US CBDC Ban Becomes Law Through 2030 After Trump Skips Signing Bill
items.summary: A bill banning U.S. central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) through 2030 became law automatically after President Donald Trump did not sign it, marking a major regulatory development.

items.title: Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal to Leave After 6 Years, Transitioning to Advisory Role
items.summary: Paul Grewal is departing Coinbase as chief legal officer on July 31, moving to an advisory role; legal VPs Molly Abraham and Ryan VanGrack will assume leadership as general counsel and vice chair.

items.title: Nasdaq and Kraken Partner to Launch Tokenized Stock Trading; Prediction Markets Seek $20B Valuations
items.summary: Nasdaq and Kraken are collaborating to enable trading of tokenized stocks, while prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket are reportedly pursuing $20B valuations in new fundraising talks.

items.title: Global Insurance Broker Aon Tests Stablecoin Payments for Premiums with Coinbase and Paxos
items.summary: Aon is piloting stablecoin payments for insurance premiums in a joint initiative with Coinbase and Paxos, signaling growing institutional adoption of stablecoins.

items.title: Block Reluctantly Adds Stablecoin Support Following Stripe and PayPal Despite Jack Dorsey’s Bitcoin-Only Stance
items.summary: Block (formerly Square) has joined Stripe and PayPal in adding stablecoin support, citing customer demand despite founder Jack Dorsey’s long-held belief that Bitcoin is the internet’s native money.

items.title: Bonzo Exploiter Holds $7M in ETH After $9M Hack; Wallet Saw 920 ETH Enter in Under an Hour
items.summary: The alleged exploiter of Bonzo, a crypto project reporting $9M in missing funds, now holds $7M in Ethereum, with over 920 ETH deposited into the wallet in less than an hour.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic's Mythos Model Exposes Vulnerabilities in Classified US Government Systems
items.summary: A U.S. official confirmed that Anthropic's AI model identified critical security flaws in highly sensitive government computer systems during a recent testing exercise.

items.title: OpenAI Restricts GPT-5.6 Sol Release to Trump-Approved Customers Amid Cybersecurity Review
items.summary: OpenAI is limiting access to its latest AI model, GPT-5.6 Sol, at the request of President Donald Trump's administration following cybersecurity concerns.

items.title: Apple Files Lawsuit Against OpenAI for Stealing Trade Secrets
items.summary: Apple accused OpenAI of stealing trade secrets on Friday as the company seeks to build its own hardware for ChatGPT integration.

items.title: Trump Administration Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Models
items.summary: The administration ended a weekslong ban on Anthropic's latest Claude chatbot versions after resolving cybersecurity alarms that prompted the initial restrictions.

items.title: Developers Push GPT-5.6 into Practical Tests Across 3D Environments and Games
items.summary: Developers are actively testing GPT-5.6 in practical applications ranging from 3D game environments and maps to Blender projects and mobile apps.

items.title: Nvidia's AI Chip Sales in China Stall as Huawei Takes Hardware Lead
items.summary: Nvidia faces stalled AI chip sales in China while local manufacturers like Huawei are overtaking global leaders in the hardware and computing power race.

items.title: Samsung and SK Hynix Plan $518 Billion Chipmaking Hub for AI Demand
items.summary: South Korean tech giants announced a combined $518 billion investment in a new computer chip manufacturing hub to address surging AI-driven demand.

items.title: Meta Announces $9.1 Billion AI Data Center Investment in Canada
items.summary: Meta will invest over 13 billion Canadian dollars ($9.1 billion) to build its first and largest AI data center in Canada.

items.title: Wimbledon Expands AI Role Across App and Website for Match Analysis
items.summary: Wimbledon is integrating AI-powered features including match analysis chatbots and live player question answering into its digital platforms.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture Reveal New Over-the-Top Action Game
items.summary: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture announced their latest action game, described as a combination of No More Heroes and Dead Rising, during recent industry coverage.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Announces New Force of Law Warbond
items.summary: Helldivers 2 surprised fans by revealing the upcoming Force of Law Warbond for the cooperative shooter, adding new content for players.

items.title: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo Announce Nioh 3
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo announced Nioh 3 during Sony's State of Play showcase, allowing soulslike fans to dive in immediately.

items.title: Arc System Works Unveils New Marvel Fighting Game
items.summary: Arc System Works and Sony Interactive Entertainment unveiled a brand-new fighting game featuring Marvel characters and tag battles.

items.title: Developer ArtPlay Reveals Bloodstained: The Scarlet Engagement
items.summary: Developer ArtPlay revealed The Scarlet Engagement, the latest entry in the Bloodstained series, during the June PlayStation State of Play.

items.title: Sony Reveals New PlayStation Controller with Unexpected Design
items.summary: Among announcements during the latest PlayStation State of Play, Sony revealed a new controller featuring an unexpected design.

items.title: Maze Theory and Vertigo Games Announce VR Stealth Franchise Return
items.summary: Maze Theory and Vertigo Games announced the return of a classic stealth franchise for VR during Sony's State of Play June showcase.

items.title: XDefiant Executive Producer Steps Away from Industry
items.summary: As fans say goodbye to XDefiant after just one year, the game's executive producer announced he will be stepping away from the industry for good.

items.title: Microsoft Unveils Xbox Game Pass Games for June 2025
items.summary: Microsoft unveiled the full list of games coming to Xbox Game Pass in the first wave of June 2025 ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase.

items.title: Pokemon GO Brings Back Community Day Voting for August 2025
items.summary: Pokemon GO brought back Community Day voting for its upcoming August 2025 edition, with four Pokemon as candidates for the spotlight.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Files Trade Secret Theft Lawsuit Against OpenAI
items.summary: Apple sued OpenAI alleging that former Apple employees stole trade secrets for OpenAI's benefit, claiming OpenAI's hardware business relies illegally on stolen secrets and that leadership normalized misconduct.

items.title: Meta Discontinues AI Image Feature Using Public Instagram Accounts
items.summary: Meta will stop a feature allowing users to generate images in Meta AI using public Instagram accounts after facing days of criticism over privacy and consent concerns.

items.title: SK Hynix CEO Predicts Worst Memory Supply Shortage Starting 2027
items.summary: SK Hynix CEO Kwak Noh-Jung warned the memory industry faces its worst-ever supply shortage in 2027, with demand expected to outstrip supply beyond 2030.

items.title: Trump Administration Intervenes to Aid Intel with Capacity Expansion and Apple Partnerships
items.summary: Sources reveal the Trump administration pushed Intel to expand local manufacturing capacity and pressured Apple to use Intel's fabrication plants to support the chipmaker's improving business.

items.title: U.S. Loosens AI Chip Export Controls to UAE for Apple, Meta, and xAI
items.summary: The Department of Commerce removed license requirements for exporting AI chips to the UAE, allowing companies like Apple, Meta, and xAI to ship chips to G42 without a license.

items.title: Netflix Plans to Add Live TV Channels to Boost Engagement
items.summary: Netflix reportedly intends to bundle competing streaming services and add live TV channels to its platform, similar to Prime Video and Apple TV, to increase user engagement.

items.title: FCC Approves Reflect Orbital's Controversial Space Mirror Satellite
items.summary: The FCC approved Reflect Orbital's Earendil-1 satellite featuring a giant mirror, stating the controversial aspect falls outside its regulatory authority focused on radio spectrum.

items.title: id Software Tries to Ease Concerns After Xbox Layoffs
items.summary: Doom Studio id Software is addressing concerns about the studio's survival following deep layoffs tied to Microsoft's cost-cutting decision.
